title: STANDARD FOR EVAPORATED MILKS CXS 281-1971 Adopted in 1971. Revised in 1999. Amended in 2010, 2018, 2022, 2023.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR EVAPORATED MILKS CXS 281-1971 Adopted in 1971. Revised in 1999. Amended in 2010, 2018, 2022, 2023.
sections.summary: Document title and revision history: the Standard for Evaporated Milks (CXS 281-1971) with adoption in 1971 and revisions/amendments through 1999, 2010, 2018, 2022 and 2023.
Notifies that amendments were made in 2022 to Section 7.5 on labelling of non-retail containers following the Codex Alimentarius Commission decisions in December 2022.

Notifies that 2023 amendments aligned the standard's food additive provisions with the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, CXS 192-1995) as part of the GSFA alignment process after the December 2023 Codex decisions.
Scope: the standard applies to evaporated milks intended for direct consumption or further processing, conforming to the product description in Section 2.

Defines evaporated milks as milk products produced by partial removal of water from milk (by heat or equivalent processes) with fat and/or protein allowed to be adjusted only to meet Section 3 compositional requirements and without altering the milk's whey protein-to-casein ratio.

Specifies permitted raw materials (milk, milk powders, cream and milkfat products), protein-adjustment products (milk retentate, milk permeate, lactose), and permitted ingredients (potable water, sodium chloride), and sets compositional minimums/maximums for evaporated milk categories (e.g., evaporated milk: min 7.5% milkfat and 25% milk solids; evaporated skimmed: max 1% milkfat and min 20% solids; partly skimmed: >1% and <7.5% milkfat and min 20% solids; high‑fat: min 15% milkfat and min 11.5% solids-not-fat, with minimum protein in milk solids‑not‑fat 34%).

Specifies permitted raw materials for the product category—milk, milk powders, cream and cream powders, and milkfat products—and allows milk retentate (milk protein concentrate by ultrafiltration), milk permeate (ultrafiltration fraction with proteins and fat removed) and lactose to be used for protein adjustment.
Permits only potable water and sodium chloride as additional ingredients for the product.

Defines composition requirements for evaporated milk subcategories with numeric limits: 'Evaporated milk' minimum milkfat 7.5% m/m, minimum milk solids 25% m/m and milk protein in solids‑not‑fat 34% m/m; 'Evaporated skimmed milk' maximum milkfat 1% m/m, minimum milk solids 20% and protein in solids‑not‑fat 34% m/m; 'Evaporated partly skimmed milk' milkfat >1% and <7.5% m/m with minimum milk solids 20% and protein in solids‑not‑fat 34% m/m; and 'Evaporated high‑fat milk' minimum milkfat 15% m/m and minimum milk solids‑not‑fat 11.5% m/m.

Restricts use of food additives to functional classes that are technologically justified for evaporated milks, permitting acidity regulators (per GSFA Tables 1 and 2 for category 01.3.1) and specified emulsifiers, firming agents, stabilizers and thickeners as indicated in the standard's table.

Requires the products and the milk used in their manufacture to comply with maximum contaminant and toxin levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193‑1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides in milk.

Recommends that preparation and handling follow Codex hygiene texts (General Principles of Food Hygiene CXC 1‑1969, Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products CXC 57‑2004) and that products comply with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21‑1997.

Introductory heading stating that additional, product‑specific labelling provisions supplement the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms; no substantive requirements in this paragraph itself.

Specifies permitted product names (Evaporated milk; Evaporated skimmed milk; Evaporated partly skimmed milk; Evaporated high‑fat milk) and allows the designation 'evaporated semi‑skimmed milk' for evaporated partly skimmed milk when milkfat is 4.0–4.5% and minimum milk solids are 24% m/m.

Requires declaration of milkfat content—when omission would mislead consumers—in a manner acceptable in the country of sale, either as a percentage by mass or volume or as grams per serving provided the number of servings is stated.

Requires declaration of milk protein content—when omission would mislead consumers—in a manner acceptable in the country of sale, either as a percentage by mass or volume or as grams per serving provided the number of servings is stated.
Permits omission from the ingredients list of milk products used solely for protein adjustment, notwithstanding Section 4.2.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods.
States that labelling of non‑retail containers should conform to the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346‑2021).
Mandates using the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234‑1999) relevant to this standard for checking compliance.

Bibliographic notes listing the Codex documents referenced in the standard (General Standards and Codes of Practice for additives, contaminants, hygiene, labelling, and methods of analysis and sampling).
